root_text.an3.5:1.3: Ayoniso pañhaṁ kattā hoti, ayoniso pañhaṁ vissajjetā hoti, parassa kho pana yoniso pañhaṁ vissajjitaṁ parimaṇḍalehi padabyañjanehi siliṭṭhehi upagatehi nābbhanumoditā hoti.
root_text.an3.5:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bālo veditabbo.
root_text.an3.5:2.1: Tīhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito veditabbo.
root_text.an3.5:2.3: Yoniso pañhaṁ kattā hoti, yoniso pañhaṁ vissajjetā hoti, parassa kho pana yoniso pañhaṁ vissajjitaṁ parimaṇḍalehi padabyañjanehi siliṭṭhehi upagatehi abbhanumoditā hoti.
root_text.an3.5:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito veditabbo.

translation_text.an3.5:1.1: “A fool is known by three things.
translation_text.an3.5:1.3: They ask a question irrationally. They answer a question irrationally. And when someone else answers a question rationally—with well-rounded, coherent, and relevant words and phrases—they disagree with it.
translation_text.an3.5:1.4: These are the three things by which a fool is known.
translation_text.an3.5:2.1: An astute person is known by three things.
translation_text.an3.5:2.3: They ask a question rationally. They answer a question rationally. And when someone else answers a question rationally—with well-rounded, coherent, and relevant words and phrases—they agree with it.
translation_text.an3.5:2.4: These are the three things by which an astute person is known.
